title: Soc Sci annual updates 20-21 (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous
Social Science
Program Review Annual Updates
2020-21
Faculty providing input: Andrea Craddock, Katie Elwood, and Chris Vancil
Principal Preparer: Valerie Roberts, Interim Dean of Liberal Arts and Student Success
While this document is designed to be an update, the full-time faculty in the Social Science
disciplines report that the Comprehensive Program Review for this area is in draft form, and to
the best of their knowledge, it was not reviewed/approved.
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
• Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, all classes in the Social Science area were abruptly
transitioned to remote instruction mid spring 2020 semester
• Two new courses were developed in the Political Science discipline – POLS 1015:
Introduction to Political Science and POLS 1012: Introduction to Political Theory. POLS
1015 was offered spring 2020.
• Biological Psychology, developed and approved at the end of spring 2019, was offered
for the first time in the spring 2020 semester.
• Dr. Andrea Craddock, psychology/sociology instructor, earned tenure.
• New part time faculty were hired for the area: 1 in history, 1 in psychology/sociology, 1
in psychology, and 1 in sociology.
• Social Science areas generated 171.49 FTES in 2019-20, as compared to 182.58 FTES in
2018-19.
• There was a 40% increase in the number of degrees offered in the Social Sciences area
from 2018-19. Degrees awarded for 2019-20 are listed below, and total 34.5% of the
degrees awarded in all discipline areas.
o History AA 3
o Psychology AA 12
o Psychology AA-T 18
o Sociology AA –T 4
o LAS Social Science 87
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
• None. Faculty have not been officially assessing SLOs or PLOs. The full time faculty
expressed a need for an institutional SLO and PLO assessment process.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Support
• The LASS Dean acquired financial support for professional development for faculty in
area

-- 1 of 2 --

title: Soc Sci annual updates 20-21 (Part 2)
text:
Obstacles
• Moving to remote instruction due to COVID-19 suddenly resulted in students 1) not
having adequate technology, 2) having to drop due to personal obligations, 3) feeling
overwhelmed by online/remote instruction. This affected enrollment.
• In order to accommodate social distancing requirements due to COVID-19, classrooms
were upgraded with zoom capability. However, this upgrade was done without faculty
input and there are resulting issues in some classrooms.
• No sociology/anthropology/ethnic studies faculty affected the number of courses,
specifically face to face, offered in these disciplines.
• Classroom availability is limited throughout campus during COS’ “peak” hours. This
limits the number of courses/sections offered.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
• The full time faculty who provided input are requesting a full time
sociology/anthropology faculty member be hired.
• Professional development is requested, to include:
o American Society for Environment History
o American Psychological Association Annual Conference
o American Political Science Association Annual Meeting
The following CQIPs will be submitted this spring:
• Full time sociology/anthropology instructor
• Increasing budget for professional development

-- 2 of 2 --
